decision: Split terminal server into its own daax-terminal image/service in the PRODUCTION deploy topology only (deploy/docker-compose.yml + Traefik). Single-container convenience modes (docker:run, rebuild.sh, root docker-compose.yml) and host-dev (bun dev) stay UNCHANGED.
rationale: F3 spec + operator GOAL: isolate the Docker-socket + workspace onto a service with its own network identity, keeping the Traefik-facing Next.js web tier socket-free. Precondition F1b (#95 WS bearer-ticket auth) is merged, so routing 4201 through Traefik to a separate service is safe.

decision: DROP docker.sock from the daax (web) service in deploy/docker-compose.yml. Web tier features that used the socket (GET /api/containers host list, /api/containers/[id]/logs, settings>Build panel live image digests via lib/build/images.ts, and SBOM via lib/sbom-syft.ts `docker run syft`) degrade to their EXISTING graceful 'Docker unavailable' 503 in the split deploy.
rationale: Operator's explicit GOAL + F3 spec intent: the internet(Traefik)-facing tier must not hold the socket (socket access == full host compromise on an in-app RCE). No proxy is built (out of scope, would be a large new attack surface). Restoring those diagnostics is a documented follow-up (read-only socket-proxy or a terminal-service internal API). Not building a proxy = smallest change satisfying the goal.

decision: Keep the runner (web) stage default CMD = start:prod (runs BOTH via concurrently) so single-container modes are unchanged. Add a distinct `terminal` build target (FROM runner, USER node, CMD start:terminal, TCP healthcheck). The production split is realized by per-service `command:`/target overrides in deploy/docker-compose.yml: daax->start:web (Next.js only, no socket), terminal->daax-terminal image (socket+workspace).
rationale: Interpreting 'drop terminal from the web CMD' at the compose-SERVICE level rather than the image default preserves the documented single-container docker:run/rebuild.sh modes (they run one container that must serve both planes). terminal FROM runner guarantees no missing transitive file vs a hand-pruned copy set.

decision: Terminal service holds: docker.sock (group_add DOCKER_GID + no-new-privileges + cap_drop ALL + boot preflight), /workspace (RW), HOST_WORKSPACE_PATH, DOCKER_NETWORK, DEFAULT_MOUNT_PATH, CLAUDE_CONTAINER_IMAGE, DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET (verify side), DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH, TERMINAL_HOST=0.0.0.0; publishes 127.0.0.1:4201 for Traefik. It needs NO Postgres (server/ imports no lib/db|pg) and NO claude-config mount (it manages auth inside /workspace/.daax). Web service keeps /workspace + claude config + Postgres and mints tickets (shares the same DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET). WS flow across containers: browser->Traefik /ws->terminal:4201 (ticket path, stateless HMAC); web never dials terminal for WS. Web /api/health now probes terminal via new TERMINAL_INTERNAL_HOST env (default 127.0.0.1; set to `terminal` in deploy).
rationale: Verified terminal env/imports empirically (grep of process.env + import graph under server/).

decision: KEEP the separate daax-terminal image (operator explicitly asked for it). Wired end-to-end: (a) deploy/docker-compose.yml builds it locally (target: terminal, same as daax:latest is built locally there); (b) .github/workflows/ci.yml gains a container-build job that smoke-builds BOTH runner+terminal targets on every PR (push:false, DAAX_SKIP_SBOM=1) so a broken terminal stage fails CI; (c) .github/workflows/publish-images.yml gains a daax-terminal publish job (mirror of daax-web, --target terminal, ghcr.io/daax-dev/daax-terminal) so registry-pull deploys (deploy:kinsale/muckross) can fetch it.
rationale: Review flagged that the terminal stage was never built in CI (could break undetected) and GHCR held a half-split. SBOM matrix left unchanged: the terminal image is the runner base + a different CMD, so its dependency SBOM equals daax-web's.

decision: Extended tests/deploy/nonroot-hardening.test.ts: assert deploy daax+terminal both set DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET from the SAME ${DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET} variable (guards cross-container ticket auth against a silent secret-mismatch), and assert the deploy daax (web) command runs start:web and NOT start:terminal/start:prod (complements the socket-absence assertion).
rationale: Nothing previously asserted the split's load-bearing auth wiring.

decision: Mount ONE shared named volume `daax-recordings` at /home/node/.daax on BOTH the daax (web) and terminal services in deploy/docker-compose.yml, and pre-create /home/node/.daax node-owned in the Dockerfile runner stage (same pattern/rationale as /app/data). Root docker-compose.yml is unchanged (combined single container — recorder and reader share one filesystem). Guarded by tests/deploy/recordings-shared-volume.test.ts.
rationale: Review-confirmed defect: the recorder (terminal plane, server/config/constants.ts RECORDINGS_DIR) writes ~/.daax/recordings while GET /api/terminal-recordings reads the same homedir path in the WEB container; post-split the web plane saw an empty dir and silently returned {recordings: []}, and recordings died with the terminal container. A named volume fixes both visibility and persistence; node-ownership pre-create prevents a root:root fresh-volume EACCES.

decision: All docker-CLI-backed web routes now detect daemon-unavailable and return the SAME structured 503 shape as GET /api/containers (error/details/hint) instead of raw 500s or misreports: GET /api/ai/active-sessions, DELETE /api/ai/active-sessions/[name], POST /api/ai/active-sessions/reap, GET /api/docker/images (was: every image misreported 'not found'), POST /api/docker/pull and POST /api/releases/[id]/build (both preflight `docker version` before streaming/mutating state). Shared classifier+response helpers live in lib/docker-exec.ts (isDockerUnavailableError, dockerUnavailableJson). The acknowledged-degradation list (CLAUDE.md split section) now names the active-sessions/reap surface explicitly with the manual fallback: `docker ps -a --filter name=daax-` / `docker rm -f daax-<8 hex>` on the host.
rationale: Review-confirmed defect: the PR's degradation list covered only /api/containers*, logs and Build digests/SBOM, while the active-sessions list/kill/reap surface — the documented cleanup path for leaked agent containers — died as HTTP 500 with a raw docker error in the split deploy. Minimal fix per review: graceful 503 + documentation; NO proxy architecture (out of scope, new attack surface).

topic: deploy scripts pull both planes (review FIX 5)
decision: deploy:kinsale / deploy:muckross now run `docker compose pull daax terminal` (was `pull daax` only).
rationale: Review-confirmed defect: the terminal image the new publish job exists for was never pulled, drifting the web and terminal planes apart although they share the lib/ws-ticket contract (same DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET HMAC).
